hero.now_serving: Now serving · Fresno · Tulare · Kern · Kings · Madera
hero.subhead: AgConn connects Central Valley farmworkers to verified seasonal jobs and CDFA-funded training programs — bilingual from day one, with a portable record of your skills that travels with you to the next harvest.

trust.line: Trusted by 2,400+ workers and 180+ verified employers across the Valley

audience.intro: Workers, employers, and training organizations each get a purpose-built experience — connected by a single record of work, in two languages, kept on file for as long as it matters.

workers.bullet1: Real seasonal jobs, verified employers, wages on record
workers.bullet2: Free CDFA & F3 training programs with bilingual certs
workers.bullet3: SMS alerts in your language. No app store, no email needed.
workers.bullet4: A skills wallet that travels with you, harvest to harvest

employers.bullet1: Bilingual job postings, ready in minutes — no rewrites
employers.bullet2: Applicant kanban: applied → reviewed → hired, with wage capture
employers.bullet3: DLSE/FLC license verification — admin-confirmed in 1 day
employers.bullet4: Worker search across a vetted pool by skill, county, availability

training.bullet1: Bilingual program listings, capacity, and waitlists
training.bullet3: WIOA-aligned exports for CDFA, F3, CalJOBS reporting
training.bullet4: SMS reminders 48h + 2h before each session, bilingual

1.label: STEP 01
1.title: Sign up with your phone
1.body: A 6-digit text gets you in. No app, no password.

2.label: STEP 02
2.title: Build your profile in minutes
2.body: Upload a resume or skip — fill in skills, county, and availability.

3.label: STEP 03
3.title: Apply to verified jobs
3.body: Filter by county, skill, wage. SMS alerts when new jobs match.

4.label: STEP 04
4.title: Earn certs that travel
4.body: Bilingual certificates land in your skills wallet. Share by link.

employer_showcase.headline: A dashboard built for the way you actually hire.
employer_showcase.body: Post a bilingual job in three minutes. Watch applicants land in a kanban — applied, reviewed, hired — with the wage you offered captured for grant reporting.

bullet1.title: Post once, in two languages
bullet1.body: The translation helper drafts the second language for you. You review, you publish.

bullet2.title: Drag to hire
bullet2.body: Move applicants across the kanban. Wage and start date capture happens at the hire step.

bullet3.title: Bulk reject without burning bridges
bullet3.body: One click rejects ten applicants. Each gets a respectful, bilingual SMS.

verification.body: Farm Labor Contractors must hold an active CA DIR/DLSE license, confirmed by AgConn staff before any posting goes live. Growers verify with a business name + EIN + county check. New employers usually clear in one business day.

bilingual.body: Every UI string, every SMS, every email, every certificate exists in both languages from byte zero. ES is reviewed by native Mexican-Spanish speakers, not auto-translated. Workers choose their language once; everything else follows.

1.title: Same page
1.body: Toggle between English and Spanish anywhere. The URL, the JSON-LD, and the OG image all switch.

2.title: Same SMS
2.body: Worker chose ES at sign-up? Every text we send arrives in Spanish. Job alerts, hire confirmations, training reminders.

3.title: Same certificate
3.body: The training certificate PDF is bilingual on the same page — English headline, Spanish body, dual dates. One artifact, two languages.

impact.headline: Real work, proved by data.
impact.body: We publish what we can — county counts, median wages, training completions, verified-employer counts — refreshed nightly. SSNs and PII never appear in any public number.

cta.dashboard: Want to dig in? Our public dashboard updates nightly.

featured_jobs.headline: Real work, posted by verified employers.

featured_training.eyebrow: LEARN A SKILL · KEEP THE CERT
featured_training.headline: Skills you keep, in writing.

testimonials.eyebrow: FROM THE PEOPLE · WHO USE IT

pricing.headline: Plans that fit your season.
pricing.footnote: All plans include FLC verification, bilingual postings, SMS alerts.

faq.headline: Questions, in plain language.
faq.intro: Real questions from real conversations with workers, FLCs, and training orgs. If something's missing, email us — we'll add it.

final.body: No credit card. No app store. No long form. Workers sign up with their phone, employers with an email.

workers.title: For workers
workers.label: Mobile phone number

workers.success: We just texted you. Open the message and tap the link.

1.quote: El primer trabajo que conseguí por aquí pagó lo que dijo. Eso ya no es común.
1.translation: The first job I found here paid what it said it would. That's not common anymore.
1.name: María R.

2.quote: Posting a bilingual job used to take me a whole afternoon. Now it's three minutes.
2.translation: Publicar un trabajo bilingüe me tomaba toda una tarde. Ahora son tres minutos.
2.name: Bronco Wine Co.

3.quote: WIOA reporting that took us a week now exports in a click. Our funders noticed.
3.translation: Los reportes WIOA que nos tomaban una semana ahora se exportan con un clic. Nuestros financiadores lo notaron.
3.name: Fresno EOC

1.q: How do I find seasonal farm work in Fresno County?
1.a: Sign up with your phone, choose your county and skills, and AgConn will text you when matching jobs go live. Every employer in the Valley list has a confirmed CA DIR/DLSE license. You can also browse jobs without signing up — only the apply step asks for your phone.

2.q: Do I need a smartphone or an app to use AgConn?
2.a: No. The web page works on any phone with a browser, and we send job alerts by SMS. There's no app to install, no app store, and no email required for workers.

3.q: What is a Farm Labor Contractor (FLC) license, and how do you verify it?
3.a: An FLC is a state-licensed labor contractor that hires farmworkers on behalf of growers. AgConn checks every FLC's license status against the CA DIR/DLSE registry before any posting goes live, and re-checks quarterly. Growers (the farms themselves) are verified by business name, EIN, and county records.

4.a: Most are. Programs funded by CDFA or F3 grants are free to workers — that's the explicit purpose of those grants. Where a program charges a fee, AgConn shows the fee on the program card before you enroll.

5.q: What does "bilingual from byte zero" actually mean?
5.a: Every interface string, every SMS, every email, and every certificate is written in both English and Spanish from the day it ships. Spanish copy is reviewed by native Mexican-Spanish speakers, not auto-translated. There is no "primary language" — they're equal citizens.

6.q: Will my information be shared with anyone?
6.a: Workers' personal information is never shared without consent. Employers see only what you choose to put on your profile, and only after you apply. Aggregate, anonymized counts (like "workers placed in Fresno County") are published on the public dashboard for grant accountability — no individual is ever identifiable.

7.q: What if my county isn't on the list?
7.a: AgConn launched in Fresno, Tulare, Kern, Kings, and Madera. We expand to new counties as we sign up partner organizations there. Tell us your county and we'll add it to the waitlist — when we open the next county, you'll get the first text.

8.a: AgConn is a California Public Benefit Corporation funded by foundation grants (initially F3) and modest paid plans for employers (Pro and Enterprise). Workers and training organizations always use AgConn for free. Our financials are summarized annually on the About page.
